description_long: This object is a rectangular stone chest or ossuary with a pitched lid, crafted from a light-colored stone, likely marble or limestone. The front face features a detailed bas-relief depicting a human figure with animals, possibly a hunting scene, while the opposite side shows a group of three human figures holding hands. The short sides are adorned with additional carvings, including a single standing figure and a circular floral motif. A continuous band of interlacing geometric patterns runs just below the lid on all sides, adding decorative complexity. The stone block sits on a flat rectangular base, and the surface appears slightly weathered but largely intact, with some minor chipping and discoloration.
description: A carved stone chest with a pitched lid, featuring bas-relief scenes of humans and animals, geometric patterns, and floral motifs, all set on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: The front view displays a bas-relief of a human figure with animals and a decorative geometric band beneath the lid.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the pitched lid's end, a circular floral motif, and faint inscriptions or carvings below the geometric band.
description_view_2: The back view features three human figures holding hands in bas-relief, with the same geometric band running above.
description_view_3: The right side view presents a single standing human figure and the end of the pitched lid with a floral motif.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: set on a prepared stone or concrete base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor chipping and some surface discoloration are visible, but no significant erosion or moss growth.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Present; includes bas-relief scenes of humans and animals, geometric bands, floral motifs, and possible inscriptions.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, displayed as a standalone monument or artifact
